FL.lienPriority: Superior to all other liens except prior tax liens
FL.saleProcess: Tax certificate sold at auction. After 2+ years, holder can apply for tax deed. No redemption after deed issuance.

TX.lienPriority: First lien superior to all other liens
TX.saleProcess: Tax lien or deed sale. Property can be redeemed within 6 months (or longer in some cases) by paying full amount + penalty + interest.
TX.source: Texas Tax Code Title 1, Subtitle E

GA.saleProcess: Tax deed sale after notice. 12-month redemption period in most cases before final deed.
GA.source: Official Code of Georgia Annotated, Title 48, Chapter 4

IL.saleProcess: Tax lien sale. 2-year redemption period. After redemption period, holder can petition for deed.

NJ.lienPriority: First lien superior to mortgages and other liens
NJ.saleProcess: Tax lien certificate sold. 2-year redemption period. After period, holder can foreclose.
NJ.source: New Jersey Statutes Annotated, Title 54, Chapter 5

CA.lienPriority: Lien for taxes is prior to most other liens
CA.interestRateCap: Varies by county; penalties per statute
CA.saleProcess: Tax-defaulted property may be sold at public auction after notice and redemption period.

CO.lienPriority: Tax lien has priority over most other encumbrances
CO.saleProcess: Tax lien certificate sale; redemption before deed in most cases.
